summary: Matched part of `HSD_TObj` by moving `MakeTextureMtx` from `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.s` into C, adding the needed Dolphin GX/MTX declarations, and renaming `func_8037A120` to the descriptive `HSD_MkRotationMtx`. The PR also introduced a large `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s` slice for remaining/non-C matrix baselib code and adjusted callsites in fighter and background flash assembly to use the new symbol name. The body notes the work was initially non-matching because the author chose not to extern floats; the final review was approved as up-to-date and OK.

key_files.why: Added C implementation of `MakeTextureMtx`, including repeat assertions, scale/rotation/translation setup, GX mirror handling, and MTX/HSD matrix calls.
Removed the old assembly body for `MakeTextureMtx` and replaced deleted float labels with `.set` aliases into the C-generated sdata2 region.
Updated the matrix header prototype to `HSD_MkRotationMtx(Mtx, Vec*)`.
Added a large assembly slice for baselib matrix routines including `HSD_MtxInverseConcat`, `HSD_MkRotationMtx`, allocation helpers, and related functions.
Added Dolphin MTX C/PS prototypes and aliases such as `MTXTrans`, `MTXConcat`, `MTXScale`, and rotation helpers needed by the C code.
Added GX texture matrix/map/wrap types and basic GX structs/constants, including `GX_MIRROR` used by `MakeTextureMtx`.
Removed duplicated generic MTX prototypes after adding more appropriate Dolphin MTX headers; retained unresolved baselib declarations where still needed.
Added `FLT_EPSILON` constant used in the texture matrix scale-zero checks.

what_changed: Implemented `MakeTextureMtx` in C with local `Vec scale`, `Mtx m`, `Vec trans`, `Quaternion rot`, and an assertion that `tobj->repeat_s && tobj->repeat_t`.

Matched texture-matrix behavior by computing scale from repeat counts divided by `tobj->scale`, guarding near-zero scales with `__fabsf(...) < FLT_EPSILON`, negating Z rotation, negating X/Y translation, applying `GX_MIRROR` wrap adjustment to Y translation, then applying translate, rotation, and scale via MTX calls.
Renamed `func_8037A120` to `HSD_MkRotationMtx` in C, baselib header, and external assembly callsites.
Added `include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h` with GX texture matrix IDs, texture map IDs, post-transform texture matrix IDs, wrap modes, FIFO/render/light/color structs, and GX boolean/enable constants.
Added/expanded `include/dolphin/mtx.h` with C_MTX and PSMTX prototypes plus `MTX*` aliases to the paired-single implementations.
Added a large `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s` file for baselib matrix routines still represented in assembly.
Cleaned `include/functions.h` by removing duplicate generic PSMTX prototypes now covered by Dolphin MTX headers.

Replaced deleted `tobj.s` sdata2 float objects with `.set` aliases such as `tobj_c_sdata2_start`, `lbl_804DE3EC`, `lbl_804DE3F0`, and `lbl_804DE3F8`, preserving references while allowing C-owned constants.
smart_moves: Used a descriptive baselib name, `HSD_MkRotationMtx`, and propagated it through headers and assembly callsites instead of leaving `func_8037A120` anonymous.
Introduced proper Dolphin GX and MTX headers rather than keeping broad declarations in `include/functions.h`, making future texture/matrix decomp work less dependent on catch-all prototypes.

Avoided externing floats by letting the C translation own constants and patching legacy assembly references with `.set` aliases; this is a useful tactic when matching C code would otherwise be distorted by forced external float labels.
Preserved the exact original assertion string `"tobj->repeat_s && tobj->repeat_t"` and line number evidence (`589` / `0x24d`) from the removed assembly body.
Used MTX macro aliases (`MTXTrans`, `MTXConcat`, `MTXScale`) while declaring them as PSMTX-backed, matching Dolphin naming patterns and keeping the C code readable.
Kept remaining matrix routines in assembly in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s`, limiting the C conversion to the function being matched rather than forcing a full baselib mtx conversion at once.
decomp_lessons: For HSD texture-object matrix code, `repeat_s`, `repeat_t`, `scale`, `rotate`, `translate`, and `wrap_t` interact directly in the matrix construction; matching depends on reproducing the exact order: translate, concatenate rotation, scale, concatenate again.
When a function uses file-local constants that were previously in assembly sdata2, `.set` aliases can bridge old labels to C-emitted constants and avoid non-matching extern-float workarounds.

Prefer moving SDK-style declarations into `include/dolphin/...` headers instead of accumulating duplicates in `include/functions.h`; this improves type consistency and reuse across unrelated callsites.
Renaming a known baselib helper should include assembly callsite updates, not just C/header changes, so searchability and linker symbols stay coherent across unmatched files.

Small struct field signedness can matter for matching and comparisons; this PR changed `HSD_TObj.wrap_t` to `s32`, likely to better match the generated compare against `GX_MIRROR` in `MakeTextureMtx`. Evidence for the exact rationale is not present beyond the diff.

A PR can start as non-matching due to float handling decisions but still be useful once the constant-placement strategy is resolved; the PR body explicitly mentions avoiding extern floats as an early blocker.
naming_conventions: Known HSD baselib matrix helpers should use `HSD_` names when identified, e.g. `func_8037A120` -> `HSD_MkRotationMtx`.
Dolphin matrix API declarations follow paired C/PS prefixes (`C_MTX*`, `PSMTX*`) with `MTX*` macros aliasing to `PSMTX*`.
GX types use SDK-like names such as `GXTexMtx`, `GXTexMapID`, `GXPTTexMtx`, `GXTexWrapMode`, `GX_TEXMTX0`, `GX_TEXMAP_NULL`, and `GX_MIRROR`.
Texture object fields retain concise HSD naming: `repeat_s`, `repeat_t`, `wrap_s`, `wrap_t`, `rotate`, `scale`, `translate`, `mtx`.
Temporary sdata2 aliasing used a descriptive anchor name, `tobj_c_sdata2_start`, plus original labels as offsets from that anchor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed only the matched `MakeTextureMtx` assembly from `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.s`, leaving the rest of `tobj.s` intact.
Replaced deleted `tobj.s` float data labels with `.set` aliases to maintain references without reintroducing duplicate constants.
Used `FLT_EPSILON` plus `__fabsf` ternaries to match near-zero float branch behavior for scale calculations.

Preserved call ordering and in-place destination behavior: `MTXTrans(tobj->mtx, ...)`, `HSD_MkRotationMtx(m, &rot)`, `MTXConcat(m, tobj->mtx, tobj->mtx)`, `MTXScale(m, ...)`, `MTXConcat(m, tobj->mtx, tobj->mtx)`.
Updated existing assembly callsites in `ft_unknown_006.s` and `lbbgflash.s` to branch-link to the renamed `HSD_MkRotationMtx`, proving the symbol was shared outside `tobj`.
Added `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s` as a large assembly coverage file for matrix routines not yet fully decompiled, keeping the build/link map consistent while progressing TObj.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the slice.
One approving review by ribbanya said: "Up-to-date and `OK`."
The PR body records an early caveat: the work was non-matching at first because the author decided not to extern floats and opened the PR for posterity while planning to finish the full file.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching `src/sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.c` after `MakeTextureMtx`.
Search for subsequent cleanup or C conversion of functions introduced in `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.s`, especially `HSD_MtxInverseConcat`.
Check whether `FLT_EPSILON` in `include/math.h` was later renamed, corrected, or moved to a standard float header.
Search later PRs for more `func_8037A120` references to confirm `HSD_MkRotationMtx` naming propagated completely.
Look for PRs that reuse the `.set` alias technique for C-emitted float constants replacing assembly sdata2 labels.
